## CONTACTS (2)

- Alex Somerville (id 35, user / self at ?)

## PROJECTS (5)

- Reprise (id 210, active, high priority)
- Harvest Dashboard (id 216, active, medium priority)

## DECISIONS (9)
- Defer item restrictions by postal code (id 4, decided 2026-03-04)
    rationale: Jessica reconsidered: "if there are more shoppers than growers, it would not matter if all growers are growing kale." Market saturation may self-correct.
- FB Marketplace-style geo privacy (id 6, decided 2026-03-12)
    rationale: Adapts proven FB Marketplace pattern for hyperlocal context. Tighter search radius (1/5/10/25 km). Jessica responded positively, raised question about dropping postal codes entirely.
- Trust-based authentication (id 1, decided 2026-03-04)
    rationale: Jessica philosophy: users should not be afraid to fail or be penalized. Verification adds friction that would hurt early adoption for a soft launch.
- Stripe over Shopify for payments (id 3, decided 2026-03-02)
    rationale: Stripe is lighter weight, lower overhead, direct integration. No need for full ecommerce platform for a single membership product.
- Embed fact details, not full threads (id 11, decided 2026-03-08)
    rationale: Dramatically better retrieval precision vs embedding entire conversation threads.
- Ollama serial request queue (id 13, decided 2026-03-08)
    rationale: Ollama can only serve one model at a time. Model-swap during concurrent calls causes server errors. All work is sequential.
- Visual-only audio player (no actual files) (id 34, decided 2026-03-06)
    rationale: Placeholder until actual demo reel recordings are uploaded. Prevents broken audio controls on live site.
- League detection: drop isZoneWide requirement (id 40, decided 2026-03-15)
    rationale: Staff enter leagues as a single lane (e.g. "BLACK 1") with merged cells spanning 4 rows, not "BLACK WHOLE". The API only returns the value in the first cell of a merge, so isZoneWide was never true fo

## PROJECTS (2)
- ARIA (id 205, active, high priority)
- paperclip-research (id 214, active, medium priority)

## DECISIONS (4)
- Embed fact details, not full threads (id 11, decided 2026-03-08)
    rationale: Dramatically better retrieval precision vs embedding entire conversation threads.
- gemma3:4b over gemma3:12b (id 12, decided 2026-03-08)
    rationale: Reduced from 12b for latency. 4b adequate for fact extraction and conflict reasoning. 2-4 min per generation on M4 MacBook Air.
- Ollama serial request queue (id 13, decided 2026-03-08)
    rationale: Ollama can only serve one model at a time. Model-swap during concurrent calls causes server errors. All work is sequential.
- Templates over AI-generated HTML (id 24, decided 2026-02-01)
    rationale: QA-able, fast, removes LLM variability. Templates are version-controlled and testable.
